meta.title: Teaching Jobs Past Paper – 100 MCQs
meta.description: 100 real past-paper style MCQs for PST, JEST, SST and Educator jobs in Pakistan.
meta.keywords: teaching jobs past paper, pst jest sst mcqs, educator test pakistan, nts ppsc spsc fpsc

subcategories.title: Teaching Jobs Past Paper 1– Full Length (65 MCQs)

questions.q: Founder of Pakistan is:
questions.ex: Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah founded Pakistan.

questions.q: Pakistan came into being on:
questions.ex: Pakistan was established on 14 August 1947.

questions.q: National language of Pakistan is:
questions.ex: Urdu is the national language of Pakistan.

questions.q: Capital of Pakistan is:
questions.ex: Islamabad is the capital.

questions.q: Largest province by area is:
questions.ex: Balochistan is largest by area.

questions.q: How many provinces are there in Pakistan?
questions.ex: Pakistan has four provinces.

questions.q: First constitution of Pakistan was enforced in:
questions.ex: First constitution was enforced in 1956.

questions.q: National flower of Pakistan is:
questions.ex: Jasmine is the national flower.

questions.q: Indus River originates from:
questions.ex: Indus River originates from Tibet (China).

questions.q: Minar-e-Pakistan is located in:
questions.ex: Minar-e-Pakistan is in Lahore.

questions.q: First Surah of Holy Quran is:
questions.ex: Al-Fatiha is the first Surah.

questions.q: Total pillars of Islam are:
questions.ex: Islam has five pillars.

questions.q: Last Prophet of Islam is:
questions.ex: Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH) is the last prophet.

questions.q: Zakat is _____ pillar of Islam.
questions.ex: Zakat is the third pillar.

questions.q: Hijri calendar is based on:
questions.ex: Islamic calendar is lunar.

questions.q: Synonym of 'Brave' is:
questions.ex: Courageous means brave.

questions.q: Antonym of 'Success' is:
questions.ex: Failure is opposite of success.

questions.q: Correct spelling is:
questions.ex: Correct spelling is Environment.

questions.q: He _____ playing cricket.
questions.ex: Correct helping verb is 'is'.

questions.q: Past tense of 'Go' is:
questions.ex: Past tense of go is went.

questions.q: Square of 15 is:

questions.ex: 1000 divided by 20 equals 50.

questions.q: Average of 10, 20 and 30 is:
Bloom’s Taxonomy is related to:
questions.ex: Bloom’s taxonomy categorizes learning objectives.

questions.q: Teaching method focused on students is:
questions.ex: Discussion method is student-centered.

questions.q: Lesson plan helps teacher to:
questions.ex: Lesson plan ensures effective teaching.

questions.q: Continuous assessment means:
questions.ex: It is ongoing evaluation.

questions.q: AV aids are used to:
questions.ex: AV aids improve understanding.

questions.q: Who presented the Pakistan Resolution in 1940?
questions.ex: A.K. Fazlul Haq presented the resolution.

questions.q: National animal of Pakistan is:
questions.ex: Markhor is the national animal.

questions.q: Pakistan’s national poet is:
questions.ex: Allama Iqbal is national poet.

questions.q: Which city is called City of Lights?
questions.ex: Karachi is City of Lights.

questions.q: Pakistan shares longest border with:
questions.ex: Pakistan shares longest border with India.

questions.q: Total Surahs in Holy Quran are:
questions.ex: There are 114 Surahs.

questions.q: First mosque in Islam is:
questions.ex: Masjid-e-Quba was first mosque.

questions.q: Namaz-e-Fajr has _____ rakats.
questions.ex: Fajr has 2 rakats.

questions.q: Which angel delivered revelations?
questions.ex: Angel Jibrael delivered revelations.

questions.q: Hajj is performed in:
questions.ex: Hajj is performed in Makkah.

questions.q: Choose correct sentence:
questions.ex: Correct sentence uses 'goes'.

questions.q: Antonym of 'Brave' is:
questions.ex: Coward is opposite of brave.

questions.q: Synonym of 'Happy' is:
questions.ex: Joyful means happy.

questions.q: Correct plural of 'Child' is:
questions.ex: Plural of child is children.

questions.q: She _____ a book yesterday.
questions.ex: Past tense of read (pronounced red) is read.

questions.q: Cube of 3 is:

questions.ex: 500 divided by 10 equals 50.

questions.q: Which is an even number?
questions.ex: 8 is an even number.

questions.q: Teaching focused on learner needs is called:
questions.ex: Student-centered teaching focuses on learners.

questions.q: Audio visual aids include:
questions.ex: Projector is AV aid.

questions.q: Evaluation means:
questions.ex: Evaluation means assessment.

questions.q: Lesson objectives should be:
questions.ex: Objectives should be measurable.

questions.q: Question-answer method is used to:
questions.ex: It checks students’ understanding.

questions.q: National game of Pakistan is:
questions.ex: Hockey is national game.

questions.q: Highest mountain of Pakistan is:
questions.ex: K2 is highest mountain.

questions.q: Independence Day of Pakistan is celebrated on:
questions.ex: 14 August is Independence Day.

questions.q: Pakistan is an _____ country.
questions.ex: Pakistan is Islamic republic.

questions.q: First Prime Minister of Pakistan was:
questions.ex: Liaquat Ali Khan was first PM.

questions.q: Which province has largest population?
questions.ex: Punjab has largest population.

questions.q: Which sea lies south of Pakistan?
questions.ex: Arab Sea lies south of Pakistan.

questions.q: Who wrote Pakistan national anthem lyrics?
questions.ex: Lyrics by Hafeez Jalandhari.

questions.q: Which test body conducts Sindh teaching tests?
questions.ex: SPSC conducts Sindh tests.

questions.q: Merit-based recruitment ensures:
questions.ex: Merit ensures fairness.

questions.q: Identify the correct article: He is ___ honest man.
questions.ex: 'Honest' starts with a vowel sound, so 'an' is the correct article.

questions.q: Choose the correct tense: She ___ to school every day.
questions.ex: The simple present tense 'goes' is used for daily routines with a singular third-person subject.

questions.q: What is the synonym of 'Abundant'?
questions.ex: 'Abundant' means existing or available in large quantities, which is synonymous with 'plentiful'.

questions.q: What is the antonym of 'Transparent'?
questions.ex: 'Opaque' means not able to be seen through, making it the opposite of 'transparent'.

questions.q: In the sentence 'The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog', what part of speech is 'quick'?
questions.ex: 'Quick' modifies the noun 'fox', making it an adjective.

questions.q: Fill in the correct preposition: He is very good ___ mathematics.
questions.ex: The correct preposition to use after 'good' when referring to skills or subjects is 'at'.

questions.q: Identify the voice of the sentence: 'The letter was written by Ali.'
questions.ex: The subject is being acted upon by the verb, which indicates the passive voice.

questions.q: Choose the correctly spelled word:
questions.ex: The correct spelling includes double 'c' and double 'm'.

questions.q: What is the meaning of the idiom 'A piece of cake'?
questions.ex: The idiom 'a piece of cake' refers to a task that is very easy to accomplish.

questions.q: Complete the conditional sentence: If it rains tomorrow, we ___ inside.
questions.ex: This is a first conditional sentence, which uses the simple present in the 'if' clause and the future tense in the main clause.

questions.q: Evaluate: 4 + 5 x 2
questions.ex: According to the BODMAS/PEMDAS rule, multiplication is performed before addition: 4 + 10 = 14.

questions.q: Solve for x: x + 5 = 12
questions.ex: Subtract 5 from both sides: x = 12 - 5, so x = 7.

questions.q: What is the sum of the interior angles of a triangle?
questions.ex: The sum of the interior angles of any triangle is always 180 degrees.

questions.q: A class has 20 boys and 30 girls. What is the ratio of boys to girls?
questions.ex: The ratio is 20:30, which simplifies to 2:3 by dividing both sides by 10.

questions.q: What is 20% of 150?
Add the fractions: 1/2 + 1/4
questions.ex: Convert 1/2 to 2/4. Then 2/4 + 1/4 = 3/4.

questions.q: What is the area of a rectangle with a length of 5 cm and a width of 4 cm?
questions.ex: Area of a rectangle = length x width = 5 cm x 4 cm = 20 cm squared.

questions.q: Expand the algebraic expression: (a + b)^2
questions.ex: The standard algebraic expansion for the square of a binomial sum is a^2 + 2ab + b^2.

questions.q: A shopkeeper bought an item for Rs. 100 and sold it for Rs. 120. What is his profit percentage?
questions.ex: Profit = 120 - 100 = 20. Profit percentage = (20 / 100) * 100 = 20%.

questions.q: Find the average of the numbers: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10.
What is the SI unit of Force?
questions.ex: The standard unit of force in the International System of Units (SI) is the Newton (N).

questions.q: What is the approximate speed of light in a vacuum?
questions.ex: The speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 299,792,458 m/s, or 3 x 10^8 m/s.

questions.q: The energy possessed by a body due to its motion is called:
questions.ex: Kinetic energy is the energy that an object possesses due to its motion.

questions.q: Newton's First Law of Motion is also known as the Law of:
questions.ex: Newton's First Law states that an object at rest stays at rest, which is the definition of inertia.

questions.q: Which instrument is used to measure atmospheric pressure?
questions.ex: A barometer is a scientific instrument used to measure atmospheric pressure.

questions.q: Which type of mirror is primarily used in the headlights of cars?
questions.ex: Concave mirrors are used in car headlights to reflect light in a straight, powerful beam.

questions.q: In which medium does sound travel the fastest?
questions.ex: Sound waves travel fastest through solids because the particles are closely packed together.

questions.q: Which organelle is known as the powerhouse of the cell?
questions.ex: Mitochondria generate most of the cell's supply of ATP, used as a source of chemical energy.

questions.q: Deoxygenated blood is carried from the heart to the lungs by the:
questions.ex: The pulmonary artery is the only artery that carries deoxygenated blood away from the heart to the lungs.

questions.q: The plant cell wall is primarily composed of:
questions.ex: Cellulose is a complex carbohydrate that forms the structural framework of the plant cell wall.

questions.q: What is the basic physical and functional unit of heredity?
questions.ex: A gene is a sequence of DNA or RNA that codes for a molecule that has a function, acting as the basic unit of heredity.

questions.q: The process by which plants make their own food is called:
questions.ex: Photosynthesis is the process used by plants to convert light energy into chemical energy to fuel their activities.

questions.q: Which vitamin is synthesized by the human skin when exposed to sunlight?
questions.ex: Vitamin D is produced in the skin from cholesterol when exposed to UVB rays from the sun.

questions.q: How many bones are there in a normal adult human body?
questions.ex: The human skeleton of an adult consists of exactly 206 bones.

questions.q: What is the chemical formula for water?
questions.ex: Water is composed of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom, giving it the formula H2O.

questions.q: What is the atomic number of Carbon?
questions.ex: Carbon has 6 protons in its nucleus, which defines its atomic number as 6.

questions.q: What is the pH value of pure water at room temperature?
questions.ex: Pure water is neutral, which corresponds to a pH of exactly 7.

questions.q: Which gas is most commonly used in fire extinguishers?
questions.ex: Carbon dioxide displaces oxygen and removes the heat from the fire triangle, making it ideal for extinguishing fires.

questions.q: Which subatomic particle carries a negative electrical charge?
questions.ex: Electrons are negatively charged subatomic particles that orbit the nucleus of an atom.

questions.q: Which is the most abundant gas in the Earth's atmosphere?
questions.ex: Nitrogen makes up about 78% of the Earth's atmosphere, making it the most abundant gas.

questions.q: Which component is considered the 'brain' of a computer?
questions.ex: The Central Processing Unit (CPU) executes instructions and acts as the brain of the computer.

questions.q: What is the MS Office keyboard shortcut to copy selected text?
questions.ex: Ctrl + C is the standard shortcut used to copy selected content to the clipboard.

questions.q: Which of the following is considered volatile memory?
questions.ex: Random Access Memory (RAM) requires power to keep its data, meaning it is volatile.

questions.q: What does the shortcut Ctrl + S do in MS Word?
questions.ex: Ctrl + S is universally used in MS Office to save the current document.

questions.q: The physical components of a computer system are known as:
questions.ex: Hardware refers to the tangible, physical parts of a computer, such as the monitor, keyboard, and motherboard.

questions.q: In computer networking, what does LAN stand for?
questions.ex: LAN stands for Local Area Network, a network that spans a small geographic area like a home or office.

questions.q: Software designed to damage or disrupt a computer system is called:
questions.ex: Malware (malicious software) includes viruses, worms, and ransomware intended to harm devices.

questions.q: What does 'IP' stand for in 'IP Address'?
questions.ex: IP stands for Internet Protocol, which dictates the format of data sent over the internet or local network.

questions.q: Which networking device forwards data packets between computer networks?
questions.ex: A router connects different networks together and directs data traffic between them.

questions.q: What is the keyboard shortcut to 'Undo' an action in MS Office?
questions.ex: Ctrl + Z reverses the last action performed in most software applications.

questions.q: Who was the first Governor-General of Pakistan?
questions.ex: Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah took the oath as the first Governor-General of Pakistan on August 15, 1947.

questions.q: Which Mughal Emperor built the Badshahi Mosque in Lahore?
questions.ex: The Badshahi Mosque was commissioned by the Mughal Emperor Aurangzeb and completed in 1673.

questions.q: Which is the largest continent in the world by land area?
questions.ex: Asia is the largest continent, covering about 30% of Earth's total land area.

questions.q: What is the first month of the Islamic calendar?
questions.ex: Muharram is the first month of the Islamic lunar calendar.

questions.q: Who is considered the National Poet of Pakistan?
questions.ex: Allama Iqbal is celebrated as the spiritual father of Pakistan and its national poet.

questions.q: What is the capital city of Australia?
questions.ex: Canberra is the capital of Australia, chosen as a compromise between Sydney and Melbourne.

questions.q: The Holy Quran was revealed over a period of approximately how many years?
questions.ex: The Quran was revealed to Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) gradually over a period of about 23 years.

questions.q: When was the 1973 Constitution of Pakistan officially promulgated?
questions.ex: The 1973 constitution was enforced on August 14, 1973, establishing a parliamentary democracy.

questions.q: Which river is generally considered the longest in the world?
questions.ex: The Nile River in northeastern Africa is traditionally considered the longest river globally.

questions.q: What is the second pillar of Islam?
questions.ex: Salah, or performing the five daily prayers, is the second pillar of Islam.
